mission.title: harbor: Enabling OIDC causes all robot accounts to generate error: failed to verify secret, username: robot$gitlab, error: failed to get oidc user info, error: <QuerySeter> no row
mission.description: Enabling OIDC causes all robot accounts to generate error: failed to verify secret, username: robot$gitlab, error: failed to get oidc user info, error: <QuerySeter> no row. Users encounter: "failed to get oidc user info, error: <QuerySeter> no row `".

steps.title: Identify harbor troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your harbor deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `failed to get oidc user info, error: <QuerySeter> no row ``

steps.title: Review harbor configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor
```
It appears when OIDC is enabled the auth goes through OIDC even if the account is a robot account with no OIDC.

steps.title: Upgrade Harbor to include the OIDC robot account fix
steps.description:
Upgrade Harbor to a version that includes the fix for robot accounts with OIDC:
```bash
# Update the Harbor Helm repo
helm repo update harbor

# Upgrade Harbor (use the version that includes the fix)
helm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor -n harbor -f values.yaml

# Verify pods restart successfully
kubectl rollout status deployment/harbor-core -n harbor
```
After upgrading, verify robot accounts can authenticate without OIDC errors.

steps.title: Confirm Enabling OIDC causes all robot accounts to… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `failed to get oidc user info, error: <QuerySeter> no row `` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: When OIDC authentication is enabled in Harbor, robot accounts fail with 'failed to get oidc user info' because the auth middleware incorrectly routes robot account credentials through the OIDC verification path. Robot accounts do not have OIDC entries in the database, causing a 'no row found' error. The fix ensures that robot account authentication bypasses the OIDC verification middleware and uses the robot token verification path instead.
codeSnippets:
# Upgrade Harbor to a version with the OIDC robot account fix
helm repo update harbor
helm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor -n harbor -f values.yaml

# Verify pods restart successfully
kubectl rollout status deployment/harbor-core -n harbor

# Test robot account authentication after upgrade
docker login <harbor-host> -u 'robot$<account-name>' -p '<robot-secret>'

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
